content_of_post_to_answer: How do I overcome social anxiety? I can’t go outside. I overthink everything. Every time I’m outside I get nervous so easily.
answer: Social anxiety often grows because the mind treats being seen as a threat, so the goal becomes avoiding judgment rather than experiencing the moment. The strange part is that confidence usually comes after doing small uncomfortable things, not before. I’ve written more about this pattern on my profile if you want to explore it further. The fear feels permanent only because avoidance keeps giving it evidence.
